summary: Frontend-only retest of Simpro import bugs. BUG 2 (Sign in with Simpro on /login) and BUG 3 (dynamic submit label pluralization) verified PASS. BUG 1 (checkbox selection in drawer) could not be click-tested because the drawer renders an empty state even though the backend returns 60 employees for filter=all — a new UI bug. Code-level review of the BUG 1 fix in UsersManagement.jsx (lines 599-625) confirms the implementation is correct (stopPropagation on <td>, onChange on input, !disabled guard on row onClick).

ui_bugs.issue: After opening drawer and clicking 'All employees' filter (data-testid=import-filter-all), UI shows 'No employees match your filters.' even though API /api/integrations/simpro/employees?company_ids=2,3&filter=all returns 60 employees (importable_count: 0, all email_missing). The header counter reads '0 selected · 0 importable · 0 shown'. Network capture shows API call order: filter=all (60 rows) → filter=whiteboard (0 rows) → filter=whiteboard (0 rows). Filter state appears to revert to whiteboard while the toggle visually shows 'All employees' active. This blocks any human verification of disabled-row behaviour for BUG 1.

integration_issues.issue: API responds with 60 employees on filter=all, but rendered visible row count is 0. The drawer's internal filter state is desynced from what is fetched/displayed.

test_results.BUG_2_sign_in_with_simpro_on_login: PASS — [data-testid=login-with-simpro] visible, text 'Sign in with Simpro', helper text mentions Simpro, hovers cleanly.
test_results.BUG_3_dynamic_label_initial: PASS — text_content of [data-testid=import-submit-label] is 'Import 0 users' (correct plural for 0). Code at UsersManagement.jsx:667-669 uses correct ternary `selected.size === 1 ? 'user' : 'users'`.
test_results.BUG_3_dynamic_label_1_and_2_selected: NOT VERIFIED VIA CLICK — could not select rows due to empty-state bug. Code review confirms ternary is correct.
test_results.BUG_1_checkbox_click: NOT VERIFIED VIA CLICK — no rows rendered. Code review of UsersManagement.jsx:599-625 confirms: row onClick has `if (!disabled) toggleRow(e)`, td has `onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}`, checkbox uses `onChange={() => toggleRow(e)}` with `disabled={!e.importable}`. Implementation matches the spec in the review request.

action_items: INVESTIGATE: ImportUsersDrawer filter state desync — API call sequence shows filter=all (60 emp) followed by filter=whiteboard (0 emp) even after user clicks 'All employees'. Possible useEffect dependency / debounce / stale state issue. Verify the request that actually populates `employees` state is the one matching the currently-highlighted filter button.

Once empty-state bug is fixed, re-test BUG 1 click flows. NOTE: All 60 real Paneltec Simpro employees have email_missing=true (importable_count=0). Either seed a test employee with an email in Simpro, or mock importable rows for the test, otherwise only the disabled-row negative path can be verified.

Optional UX: when importable_count is 0 but count > 0, the empty state should not say 'No employees match your filters.' — it should list the disabled rows so the user can see them (and possibly show a banner explaining why none are importable).
critical_code_review_comments: UsersManagement.jsx is 678 lines — close to the 700-line soft limit. Consider extracting <ImportUsersDrawer/> into its own file.

Line 668 label text 'Import 0 users / 1 user / N users' renders correctly but the wrapping button uses Tailwind `uppercase` class, so visible text is 'IMPORT 0 USERS'. Functionally fine, but tests asserting visible text must use case-insensitive compare or .text_content() (DOM source) instead of .inner_text() (rendered).

success_rate.frontend: BUG2 PASS, BUG3 PASS (initial state + code review), BUG1 BLOCKED (cannot click-test due to drawer empty-state bug)

test_credentials: stephen@paneltec.com.au / Mcgstephen50# (admin) — login works, redirected to /app/dashboard.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Drawer renders empty even though API returns 60 employees for filter=all. Investigate src/pages/UsersManagement.jsx ImportUsersDrawer component — the filter button state vs the actual fetch state are desynced. Once fixed, the 60 real Simpro employees are all email_missing (disabled rows) — so to fully test BUG 1's importable-row checkbox click, an importable row must be seeded (either via Simpro test data or by mocking the employees endpoint to return at least 2 rows with email populated and importable=true). The previous tester noted this same data caveat; main agent should consider seeding test data for reliable CI verification.
rca_of_the_issue: Backend Simpro API for Paneltec org returns 60 employees but all have email=null, so importable_count=0. The original BUG 1 fix code is correctly applied (verified by line-by-line review of UsersManagement.jsx 599-625). However, a separate rendering bug in the drawer (filter state desync) prevents any rows from being shown, blocking interactive verification. Network trace: GET /api/integrations/simpro/employees?company_ids=2,3&filter=all → 200 with 60 rows; followed by GET ...?filter=whiteboard → 200 with 0 rows, and the UI ends up displaying the whiteboard (0-row) result while the 'All employees' filter pill remains visually selected.
